entries.title: The Light Phone Flip coming out in 2027
entries.summary: The Light Flip is a minimalist, distraction-free 5G flip phone. It is designed to reduce digital clutter by completely omitting social media, web browsers, and email. The post The Light Phone Flip coming out in 2027 first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The Light Flip is a minimalist, distraction-free 5G clamshell phone designed by Light. It strips away social media, web browsers, and infinite scrolling to help users reduce screen time. Unlike their previous phones, which have an E INK screen, this one only has an OLED.

The Light Flip features a 2.8-inch OLED screen with a resolution of 480×640 and has a matte screen protector installed at the factory to help eliminate the glare from the sun and other overhead lighting. Under the hood is a MediaTek MT8873 processor, 6GB of RAM, and 128GB of internal storage. Two microphones with noise cancellation are good for calls, and it has two stereo speakers for speakerphone. 5G and 4G LTE connectivity, the Light Flip can download podcasts online and play music that you upload to your phone, which you can listen to via either a 3.5 mm headphone jack or Bluetooth headphones. It charges via USB-C, works with both eSIM and physical Nano SIM cards, and includes a 12-megapixel camera on the back. It is powered by a 1800 mAh battery, which is fully removable with common tools and is rated IP54.

There is no touchscreen on the phone; instead, it has a user-friendly 12-button keypad that will feel nostalgic for anyone who used T9 for texting or remembers dialing on a landline. It includes three function buttons, with the middle one integrated into a four-way directional pad to easily navigate the phone’s cursor. Additionally, there’s a home button conveniently placed on the side, along with volume control switches nearby.

Why a flip phone? Light’s founder Joe Hollier and Kaiwei Tang told TechCrunch that they’ve gotten a lot of requests for the form factor, particularly from younger users who want a phone that’s even less smartphone-like than the Light Phone III. They’ve also seen lots of people in “distraction-free” communities move to old-school flip phones — but most new flip phones in production are not terribly capable.

Light offers the Flip in a range of colors: black, red, yellow, pink, navy blue, and light gray, and is available as a pre-order on their website for $299.99. It is not planned to ship until April of 2027, so it won’t be out anytime soon.

However, there are a few drawbacks that will prevent me from buying it. It has no touchscreen, selfie camera, or NFC. The body is made of plastic and doesn’t have an E-ink display. It only has a music and podcast app, but the company is promising an SDK for developers to work on third-party apps.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Audible announces New Full-Cast Adaptation of Dracula
entries.summary: Rather than a traditional audiobook, Dracula has been developed as a full-cast audio drama featuring Hollywood talent, an original score, and immersive sound design. The post Audible announces New Full-Cast Adaptation of Dracula first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
Audible has announced its forthcoming adaptation of Dracula – an Audible Original that reimagines Bram Stoker’s chilling psychological thriller as an immersive, full-cast audio experience. Launching globally on October 1, 2026, Dracula will be released in English, Castilian Spanish, French, German, Italian, Latin American Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese, with each production featuring an all-star local cast.

When her fiancé vanishes in Transylvania, Mina Murray pieces together fragments of letters, diary entries and chilling testimony to hunt the predator stalking those she loves, only to discover he’s already hunting her. When Mina’s closest friend Lucy succumbs to his thrall, the enigmatic Professor Van Helsing is summoned to wage war on this unfathomable threat. But it is Mina, with her intellect, her resolve, her gift for drawing unseen lines between clues, who may hold the key to stopping this ancient predator—if he hasn’t already found his way inside her mind.

The English-language production stars Jonathan Bailey (Wicked) as Dracula, Ella Purnell (Fallout) as Mina, Aimee Lou Wood (The White Lotus) as Lucy, Louis Partridge (Enola Holmes) as Jonathan, David Jonsson (Industry) as Dr Seward, Lesley Manville (Phantom Thread) as Mrs. Westenra and Maggie Gyllenhaal (The Dark Knight) as Professor Van Helsing.

Aurelie de Troyer, Head of Audible Content, International, said: “This is what Audible does best,crafting immersive audio experiences that transport listeners into the heart of the story. When Dracula whispers in your ear, when you hear his footsteps in the dark but see no reflection, that’s when you understand why we’re the home for this story. Our production elevates the psychological thriller at Dracula’s core, with the battle between Mina and Dracula as the heart of it all.”

She continued, “We’ve assembled world-class casts across seven languages, from Jonathan Bailey and Ella Purnell to Omar Sy, Riccardo Scamarcio and Matthias Schweighöfer, exceptional international talent delivering a truly global Dracula. With director Mary Lambert at the helm and a haunting score by Ilan Eshkeri, we’re bringing this ambitious production to life just in time for Halloween.”

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: The XTEINK X4 Pro e-reader is now available
entries.summary: The XTEINK X4 Pro is an ultra-compact, pocket-sized e-reader priced at $99 USD. Designed to combat doomscrolling by offering a distraction-free reading experience, it features a magnetic backplate that allows it to attach directly to the back of MagSafe- or Qi2-compatible smartphones. The post The XTEINK X4 Pro e-reader is now available first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The XTEINK X4 Pro e-reader is now available and ready to order from the company’s website for around $99. It is designed as a premium alternative to the original minimalist XTEINK X4; it introduces highly requested modern features while maintaining its ultra-portable, distraction-free identity. The big selling points are the new touchscreen and front-lit display, two features that are sure to impress; however, it no longer has a USB-C port.

The X4 Pro features a 4.3-inch E-Ink touchscreen with a resolution of 220 PPI. The lighting system is the most anticipated upgrade. It allows you to read comfortably in the dark or in low-light environments without needing a separate clip-on reading lamp. Despite adding layers for the light and touch sensors, the Pro model measures an incredibly slim 5.98mm, making it slightly thinner than the original X4’s 6.4mm. It retains its physical page-turn buttons alongside the new touchscreen, giving you tactile control when flipping pages.

There will be 16 GB of fixed internal memory for reading e-books, but it is an SD card and it is capable of storing up to 256GB. There is a paltry 8MB RAM and Wi-Fi (2.4 GHz) and Bluetooth. It is powered by a 1100mAh battery, which should be enough for a month of reading. Unlike the standard USB-C on the first-generation X4, the X4 Pro utilizes a magnetic bespoke/pogo-pin charging cable (using a USB-C adapter). The native software supports ebooks in TXT, EPUB, MOBI, and PDF formats, along with JPG and PNG image formats.

This device retails for $99 from the XTEINK website.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: US Audiobook Sales were up 13% in May 2026
entries.summary: E-Book revenues were down nearly 1% to $84.2 million for May 2026, while Digital Audiobook revenues were up 13.0% to $100.7 million. The post US Audiobook Sales were up 13% in May 2026 first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
E-Book revenues were down nearly 1% to $84.2 million for May 2026, while Digital Audiobook revenues were up 13.0% to $100.7 million. Physical Audio revenues were up 16.3% to $500 thousand. E-book sales have been steadily declining all year long; this is partly attributed to audiobooks becoming more popular.

Year-to-date e-book revenues were down 3.5% compared to 2025, for a total of $429.7 million. The Digital Audio format was up 14.5%, coming in at $500.4 million in revenue. Physical Audio revenues were down 6.3%, coming in at $2.2 million. You can clearly see that digital audiobooks are now regularly outselling e-books, which is a good trend.

U.S. audiobook sales revenue reached $2.43 billion in 2025, a 9% increase from the previous year, with publishers reporting over 750,000 active titles. Digital formats accounted for 99% of all sales. While human-narrated audiobooks remain dominant, AI-produced titles surged to over 40,000. In 2026, the global audiobook market is rapidly expanding, with revenues projected to hit between $11.33 billion and $14.15 billion. The industry is experiencing massive shifts in production models—most notably, the rise of “audio-first” titles created specifically with listeners in mind.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Essential Books About Video Games Every Player Should Add to Their Reading List
entries.summary: Video games are a creative entertainment product that combines tech with storytelling and art, but books offer deeper insight. If practice during the game and research through books are combined, it adds depth to your gaming experience and improves the fun. The post Essential Books About Video Games Every Player Should Add to Their Reading List first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
When it comes to video games, many players teach themselves using the learning-by-doing method. They load into the game guns blazing as rookies, fail and respawn, and overall have a good time. However, if you are truly passionate or wish to take the pro route, then you might want to go past the video game streams. The industry grew beyond the stream, and now we have novels, developer stories, cultural analyses, and more.

Those interested in Dota 2 betting outside casual play usually need this type of information to back up their wagers. The details in these books help them learn more about eSports strategies and what it takes to win a game, which ultimately adds to calculating odds. Reading as many books as possible can give you an edge that few players or bettors have.

Why Read Books on Video Games

Whether you are new to video games such as Dota 2 or are already into the competitive eSports scene, books can offer valuable information. Here are the main reasons why you should read as many books as you can.

  • You’ll Get the Lore Better: Some games are complex or long enough that they have decades’ worth of lore in them. Books can offer more insight and background than you can learn during gameplay.
  • Character Connection Gets Deeper: We rarely see the full extent of a character’s motivations and history in a game. Some books can expand on that, allowing the characters to become more meaningful.
  • You Polish Your Strategic Thinking: Books may not replace practice and experience, but they give valuable insight into matters like teamwork and strategy. Reading them lets you learn more about resource management, decision-making, and competitive psychology.
  • Hidden Details Become Visible: Developers usually leave references, clues, and world-building elements hidden in the games. Reading the books lets you catch onto details you may otherwise miss in the game.

Adding the right books to your reading list can make complex games seem less intimidating. Whether they include general practical tips or behind-the-scenes info, they can help players better understand important concepts.

Books on the Gaming Culture and Philosophy

If you are just starting with video games, then you need to read books to create your foundation. Some book ideas that guided many players at the start of their journey include:

1. The Ultimate History of Video Games by Steven L. Kent

Some call this the “Bible” of video game history, covering everything you might need to know about the gaming industry’s evolution. This book includes everything from the days of mechanical pinball and the creation of Pong to the gaming wars among Sega, Nintendo, Sony, and Microsoft.

The book features direct interviews with those who built the industry, offering first-person insight. It’s a must-read because it tells you why video games look the way they do, building respect for the roots.

2. Extra Lives: Why Video Games Matter by Tom Bissell

This book is part memoir, part cultural criticism, in which the author explains his experience as a video game enthusiast. It talks about how tools have become powerful tools with the ability to shape emotions and creativity, leading to the creation of modern storytelling.

If you ever find yourself struggling to understand why a video game or tournament made you feel a certain way, this game can offer valuable insight.

3. Reality Is Broken by Jane McGonigal

Written by a renowned game designer, this book starts with the premise that real life can be frustrating and video games were made to improve human happiness. Instead of painting them as a distraction, she presents them as a tool to improve motivation, problem-solving, and collaboration.

The book offers insight into how video game mechanics can influence everyday life decisions, and everything in it is backed up by psychological research.

Books Going Behind-the-Scenes of Video Games

If you’re looking to specialize, many authors have created books on specific game strategies and lore. Some of these books were written by developers, while others were written by players who mastered the game. Be sure to add these video game books to your reading list:

1. Blood, Sweat, and Pixels by Jason Schreier

This book is a collection of real-world stories gathered from development studios when creating games such as Diablo II, Witcher II, and Uncharted 4. It talks about the creative struggles of creating the game, personal sacrifices, and technical challenges that they had to overcome to launch the game.

Once you finish reading the book, you likely won’t be able to look at a launch-day book the same, now that you know what went into making it.

2. Masters of Doom by David Kushner

This book is a biography that follows the stories of John Carmack and John Romero, the minds behind the “Doom” and “Quake” video games. It talks about how Id Software laid the groundwork for first-person shooters and 3D graphics, completely changing competitive gaming.

It also explains how they formed the multiplayer “deathmatch” culture before their clashing personalities led them to part ways.

3. League of Legends: Realms of Runeterra by Riot Games

If your specialty is in League of Legends, then this book is a must-read, particularly if you are a beginner. Here, players can find more about the history, factions, geography, and the massive fantasy world elements of LoL. It fills the gaps that casual gameplay won’t be able to, offering more strategic insight based on surroundings.

4. Counter Strike History by Isaac Berners-Lee

For CS: GO fans, this book follows the journey of the Counter-Strike game, from Half-Life up until it became the most popular eSports title. Players can find out more about the successes and failures of the founders, game philosophies, competitive evolution, and the community impact of the game over the past two decades.

Fans should read this book because it shows how passion can shift the competitive gaming landscape in only a few years.

Video games are a creative entertainment product that combines tech with storytelling and art, but books offer deeper insight. If practice during the game and research through books are combined, it adds depth to your gaming experience and improves the fun.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Best Buy has a late Prime Day Savings on Kindle e-readers
entries.summary: Best Buy US is having a late Prime Day sale until Sunday, July 26th. Score a Kindle, Paperwhite, Paperwhite Signature Edition, and Kindle Scribe Colorsoft. The post Best Buy has a late Prime Day Savings on Kindle e-readers first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
Amazon Prime Day was last month, and there were sales on virtually every single Kindle, including the new Kindle Scribe Colorsoft. However, to take advantage of those deals, you had to be a Prime Member, which lots of people are not. If you are looking to buy a new Kindle and live in the United States, Best Buy is having the same Prime Day prices, but there is no membership required.

The Best Buy Kindle sales can be purchased online, but since the promotion launched today, the base Kindle is already sold out. So if you are dead bent on scoring one, you would have to visit a retail store. You can snag the Kindle Paperwhite for just $124.99, down from its original price of $159.99. If you’re looking for something a bit more advanced, the Signature Edition is available for $144.99. The Kindle Colorsoft starts at $159.99, while its Signature Edition version is priced at $219.99. Plus, there are special bundles for kids—get the Paperwhite kids bundle for $139.99, and the Colorsoft kids bundle for $179.99. Don’t miss out on these deals!

The latest Kindle Scribes have some great discounts! The Kindle Scribe Colorsoft is now available for $479.99, down from its original price of $629.99. Meanwhile, the black-and-white frontlit model is priced at $379.99 instead of $499.99. However, it’s worth noting that the new Kindle Scribe without a frontlight isn’t on sale—this model also missed out on discounts during Prime Day.

Visit the Best Buy Prime Day Sale.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Hisense A10 E INK Smartphone specs revealed
entries.summary: The A10 front display is 6.13 inches and uses E INK Carta 1300 and the new Qualcomm QCM6690 Dragonwing processor. The post Hisense A10 E INK Smartphone specs revealed first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
Bigme has put a dual screen smartphone on crowdfunding with an E INK screen on one side and an LCD one the back. Hisense is now doing something similar with the Hisense A10. The A10 front display is 6.13 inches and is using E INK Carta 1300 and the new Qualcomm QCM6690 “Dragonwing” processor. This has eight cores and a clock speed of 2.9 GHZ and support for  5G + 5G DSDA enabling a 5G device to maintain two active 5G connections simultaneously—one on each SIM, all at optimized power consumption. It also features Wi-Fi 7 for faster, more reliable wireless performance, and the latest Bluetooth 6.0 for enhanced data transfer speeds and reduced latency.

The A10 also supports NFC, likely to connect the removable 5 inch LCD display on the back of the phone, the rumours also say it physically connects with magnets or pogo pins. It also has wireless charging and IR transmitter that lets you use the phone as a remote control. Google Android 16 is the operating system, but it will not have Google Play, but similar to past Hisense phones, it will have a small app store with a bunch of Chinese apps, such as Wechat Reading.However, it does have support for sideloaded apps, which makes it relevant for users in North America and Europe. Typically, Hisense supports many different languages out of the box.

This phone will hit the Chinese market first, sometime in the next few months and is estimated to cost $590.00 USD. It will be imported by a bunch of other companies based in North America, so it would be easier for the average customer to order one, but it will likely cost more due for them to make a profit.

via Weibo

entries.title: The Best e-readers for August 2026
entries.summary: The last month of summer is upon us, and there are plenty of new e-readers to pick up for a day at the beach, park, or late vacation. Plenty of new devices have just come out or will come out soon, in addition to perennial favourites. The post The Best e-readers for August 2026 first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The last month of summer is upon us, and there are plenty of new e-readers to pick up for a day at the beach, park, or late vacation. Plenty of new devices have just come out or will come out soon, in addition to perennial favourites.

The Amazon Kindle Paperwhite (12th Gen) remains the best overall e-book reader due to its lightning-fast processor, crisp contrast, and immense 12-week battery life. However, the market has diversified significantly with breakthroughs in vibrant color E Ink screens and highly flexible, open Android platforms.

BOOX Tablet Go 6 Gen II (Custard)

The BOOX Go 6 (Gen II) is a compact, 6-inch pocketable black-and-white e-reader produced by Onyx International. It represents an incremental upgrade over the first-generation Go 6. The standout change is the addition of active stylus support for handwriting and note-taking, making it one of the smallest e-ink note-taking tablets available.

Sale

XTEINK X3 eBook Reader (Developer Edition), 3.7" Pocket E-Ink eReader, Ultra-Thin 0.2", 2 oz...

The XTEINK X3 is an ultra-compact, pocket-sized E-Ink eReader designed primarily for distraction-free reading on the go. Created by the specialized hardware team Xteink, it has gained viral popularity as a purse Kindle or companion reader that can magnetically mount directly to the back of a smartphone.

Bigme B6 Color E-Reader, Colour eBook Readers 6 Inch E-Ink eReaders Digital ePaper Tablet with...

The Bigme B6 is a versatile, pocket-sized 6-inch E-Ink tablet that serves as an open-ecosystem budget alternative to locked-down readers like the Kindle. Available in both Monochrome (Black & White) and Kaleido 3 Color variations, its defining trait is that it runs a full, unrestricted version of Android 14 with the Google Play Store pre-installed.

PocketBook Era Lite | Affordable 7" e-Reader | Latest E Ink Carta™ 1300 Screen | SMARTlight | Open...

The PocketBook Era Lite is a premium, mid-range 7-inch monochrome eReader running a fast, lightweight Linux operating system. Designed as a streamlined, cost-effective alternative to the standard PocketBook Era and Era Color, it focuses heavily on open-ecosystem flexibility, robust format compatibility, and ergonomic physical buttons

reMarkable Paper Pure – 10.3” Paper Tablet with Black-and-White Display – Digital Notebook for...

  • The new reMarkable Paper Pure: Close the gap between how you think and work. This paper tablet combines the proven benefits of handwriting with software that simplifies everyday work tasks, like getting your thoughts down on paper, reviewing documents, and staying focused in meetings.
  • Our best black-and-white paper tablet yet: The third-generation 10.3-inch display is whiter and has more contrast than ever before. Digital ink appears in just 21 ms — faster than the blink of an eye.
  • Your place to think: Just put your pen to paper and take notes, mark up documents, and sketch. With dozens of available note-taking templates, an uncluttered screen, and easy navigation, thoughts can flow without interruption.

Amazon Kindle Paperwhite 16GB (newest model) – 20% faster, with new 7" glare-free display and...

The Amazon Kindle Paperwhite (12th Generation) is a premium, mainstream 7-inch monochrome eReader running Amazon's highly optimized, closed ecosystem software. Released as a substantial performance refresh, it holds the title of Amazon's fastest eReader to date, featuring noticeably snappy navigation and vastly improved screen response times over the previous 11th-generation model.

Kobo Clara BW | eReader | 6” Glare-Free Touchscreen with ComfortLight PRO | Dark Mode Option...

The Kobo Clara BW is a highly portable, budget-friendly 6-inch monochrome eReader built on a streamlined, open Linux ecosystem. Released as the refined successor to the Clara 2E, its defining trait is its status as one of the first major mainstream eReaders to adopt next-generation E Ink Carta 1300 screen technology. This upgrade delivers class-leading text crispness and remarkable responsiveness for its price bracket.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Kindle Book Samples are Broken on Amazon
entries.summary: There appears to be a major bug with Kindle Books on Amazon. "Read Sample" is no longer displaying for millions of books. The post Kindle Book Samples are Broken on Amazon first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
Millions of people are having problems reading samples of Kindle books on Amazon. It does not matter if readers are using Safari, Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome, or Mozilla Firefox. The Read Sample button, which shows the first chapter of an e-book, has been a staple on Amazon for the past decade, but now there seems to be a bug in the system for both desktop, mobile, and the Amazon Kindle App.

There doesn’t appear to be any rhyme or reason why this is occurring. Some users say there is nothing wrong, but the vast majority see only the “Send Sample” option, not “Read Sample.” You can see the Read Sample that is no longer showing on the first book of the Dungeon Crawler Carl series in the main image of this post. However, a self-published title is still showing it.

It does not appear that Amazon has changed any new rules with their Kindle Direct Publishing platform. The text clearly states, “Once your book is available for purchase on Amazon, including eBook pre-orders, the reading sample will display within a few days. If you make updates to your book, it will take additional time for the sample to update with the latest content.” On average, it is between eight and ten days for it to show up, but most of the books that are no longer showing titles have been out for many months or years. However, Amazon does offer the option to remove samples or limit the number of pages it displays, but you have to contact them directly.

Update: I checked Amazon.com, Amazon.ca, and Amazon.co.uk for the same Kindle Books, and none are displaying properly.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Amazon Kindle Sale at Target from July 19th to 25th
entries.summary: Target's weekly ad running from July 19 to July 25, 2026, features a highly discussed sale on Kindle e-readers, highlighted by a notable pricing error on the Kindle Colorsoft. The post Amazon Kindle Sale at Target from July 19th to 25th first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The upcoming Target Weekly Ad for July 19th to July 25th features highly anticipated Amazon Kindle Store Deals at Target, matching Prime Day-level discounts. There is a mistake that lists the premium 32GB Kindle Colorsoft Signature Edition for the standard price of the 16GB version for $249. The digital ad’s click-through link routes shoppers to the base 16GB model instead.

Shoppers note that Target’s guest services usually honour printed ad errors if the discrepancy is under $40, meaning early buyers might score the 32GB model at an unexpected discount.

While specific discount percentages vary by region, the following models are heavily promoted for this sales cycle

Amazon Kindle 6″ e-Reader (2024): The lightest baseline model, marked down from its usual $109.99.

Amazon Kindle Paperwhite (2024): Standard and Signature Editions featuring the flush-front design and warm adjustable lighting.

Amazon Kindle Colorsoft: The newly released color e-ink displays (both 16GB and 32GB Signature editions).

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.
